1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (analogy / refactor / challenger), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	- archon-lean-lsp: Lean LSP MCP server (project scope) — use for all Lean LSP operations (search, diagnostics, goal inspection)
19	- archon-informal-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for informal mathematical reasoning
20	- archon-refactor-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-refactor-agent.py` — invoke the refactor subagent on a directive file
21	- archon-analogy-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-analogy-agent.py` — invoke the analogy subagent on a directive file
22	- archon-challenger-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-challenger-agent.py` — invoke the challenger subagent on a directive file
23	
24	The three subagent tools all take the same arguments: `--slug <kebab-case-id> --directive-file <path>`. Each shells out to `archon subagent <name> ...`, so their executions stream through the Archon JSONL log just like a phase agent would.
25	
26	## Key Files & Permissions
27	
28	All state files are in `.archon/`:
29	
30	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger) | Review Agent | User |
31	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
32	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
33	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
34	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
35	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
36	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
37	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (`<role>-<slug>.md`) | read only | read |
38	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
39	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
40	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read** (refactor may rename file path only) | read | **write** |
41	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | refactor: write (all files; protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed); analogy/challenger: read-only on project source | do not edit | write (via comments) |
42	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
43	| `Challenges/<Name>.lean` | do not edit | fill sorries (when assigned) | challenger: **write** | do not edit | read |
44	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | do not access | analogy: **write** | do not access | read |
45	
46	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow, run by hand by the mathematician.** The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. The plan agent invokes the refactor subagent by writing a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/` and passing its path to `archon-refactor-agent.py --directive-file`. This keeps each loop-driven refactor independently logged and avoids any chance of stale-directive reuse. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`, etc.) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise and prune them out as you rewrite.
47	
48	## Protected Declarations
49	
50	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
51	
52	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
53	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
54	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed.
55	
56	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
57	
58	The blueprint uses two active markers.
59	
60	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
61	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
62	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
63	
64	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between the prover and review phases each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves — let the script do it.
65	
66	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations remain the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
67	
68	## User Interaction
69	
70	Users provide hints in two places:
71	
72	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
73	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
74	
75	## Agent Roles
76	
77	### Plan Agent
78	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
79	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
80	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
81	- Optionally invoke the **subagents** (`analogy`, `refactor`, `challenger`) by shelling out to the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, **before** writing prover objectives. See `.archon/prompts/plan.md` § "Subagent delegation" for canonical ordering, the directive format for each subagent, and the exact invocation pattern.
82	- Archive any subagent report you receive to `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<role>-<slug>-report.md` so the dashboard can render it.
83	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
84	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
85	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
86	
87	### Prover Agent
88	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
89	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
90	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
91	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
92	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
93	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
94	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
95	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
96	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
97	
98	### Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger)
99	
100	These are **not** part of the autonomous loop's standing phases. The plan agent invokes them on demand by running the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, with `--slug <slug> --directive-file <path>`. Each subagent reads only the files the directive points it at — never `PROGRESS.md`, `STRATEGY.md`, or other plan-agent state — and writes a self-contained report.
101	
102	- **`refactor`** — read `.archon/prompts/refactor.md`. Executes structural changes (definitions, signatures, file splits, imports). Inserts `sorry` at broken proof sites; never fills proofs. Writes report to `.archon/task_results/refactor-<slug>.md`.
103	- **`analogy`** — read `.archon/prompts/analogy.md`. Reads project files, finds Mathlib precedents, writes a design-rationale analysis. Persistent output at `analogies/<slug>.md`; report at `.archon/task_results/analogy-<slug>.md`. Read-only on project source.
104	- **`challenger`** — read `.archon/prompts/challenger.md`. Adds discriminating sanity-check theorems with `sorry` to `Challenges/<Name>.lean`. Provers fill them later. Report at `.archon/task_results/challenger-<slug>.md`. Read-only on target files.
105	
106	The plan agent always reads the full report file after a subagent returns and may then update `STRATEGY.md` / `PROGRESS.md` based on the findings. None of the subagents spawn other subagents.
107	
108	### Review Agent
109	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
110	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
111	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
112	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
113	- Maintain the **semantic** blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE: ...` annotations, stale `\notready` removal — in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch `\leanok`; it's handled by the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase that ran before you.
114	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md
115	
116	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
117	
118	Two non-agent steps run automatically each iteration to reduce the burden on the agents above:
119	
120	- **Pre-compactors** (`compact-strategy`, `compact-task-pending`, `compact-task-done`, `compact-project-status`): run before plan / review, rewriting oversized state files in place while preserving every actionable detail. Configured under `compaction.*` in `.archon/config.json`. Skipped when files are below threshold.
121	- **`sync_leanok`**: runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status. Replaces what used to be a multi-page review-agent task.
122	
123	Both write inner-git commits (`archon[NNN/precompact/...]`, `archon[NNN/marker-sync]`) so their output is auditable and revertable.

1	# Review Agent — Post-Session Proof Journal + Analysis
2	
3	You are the review agent. Your job is to: (1) analyze the most recent prover session with fine-grained detail, (2) produce a structured proof journal, (3) update project status, (4) maintain the blueprint markers that require *semantic* judgement (`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations), and (5) write recommendations for the next plan iteration.
4	
5	`\leanok` placement is handled deterministically by Archon's `sync_leanok` phase, which runs immediately before you. **Do not add or remove `\leanok` yourself.** If a `\leanok` you expect is missing, the underlying Lean source still has a sorry or doesn't compile — investigate, don't paper over.
6	
7	**Do NOT modify any .lean files. Do NOT write proofs.** You may run `lean_diagnostic_messages` / `lake env lean <file>` to verify compilation and sorry counts as part of your analysis. Otherwise you only read logs, analyze, and write journal/status/blueprint-marker files.
8	
9	## Iteration number — canonical
10	
11	Your invocation prompt contains `Archon iteration: NNN` and `Session number: M`. As of 2026 these are the **same number** — `session_M/` is always the review of `iter-NNN` (i.e. M == NNN). Use the iteration form (`iter-NNN`) when referencing the iteration in prose (`summary.md` metadata, recommendations titles like "Recommendations for the next plan-agent iteration (iter-{NNN+1:03d})") and the bare integer (`session_M`) when referring to the review-output directory. If older `summary.md` / `recommendations.md` files use the legacy independent session counter, treat them as drift from earlier agents — your new file uses the iteration number.
12	
13	## Step 1: Identify Context
14	
15	1. Check `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/` — count existing session folders to determine the current session number.
16	2. Run `find ${PROJECT_PATH} -name '*.lean' -not -path '*/.lake/*' -not -path '*/lake-packages/*' | xargs grep -c 'sorry' 2>/dev/null | grep -v ':0$' | awk -F: '{s+=$2} END {print s}'` for the current sorry count.
17	3. Run `git diff HEAD~1 --stat` to see what changed.
18	
19	## Step 2: Read Pre-processed Attempt Data (MANDATORY)
20	
21	**READ `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` COMPLETELY.** If this file does not exist or is empty, report it and proceed with what you can gather from task_results.
22	
23	The file contains:
24	
25	- Line 1: Summary stats (`type: "summary"` — total edits, goal checks, errors, files edited)
26	- Remaining lines: One event per tool call — edits, goal states, errors, lemma searches, builds
27	
28	**For each `code_change` event**: Record the actual code that was tried (old_text → new_text).
29	**For each `goal_state` event**: Record the Lean goal at that point.
30	**For each `diagnostics` event**: Record the Lean errors.
31	**For each `build` event**: Record whether it succeeded or failed.
32	
33	This is your PRIMARY data source. Task result files are supplementary.
34	
35	## Step 3: Read Recent History
36	
37	If previous session folders exist in `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/`, read `summary.md` from the **last 2 sessions**. Also read `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` if it exists.
38	
39	## Step 4: Write Proof Journal
40	
41	Create the session folder and write two files:
42	
43	```bash
44	mkdir -p .archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_<N>
45	```
46	
47	### File A: `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_<N>/summary.md`
48	
49	Must include:
50	- Session metadata (number, sorry count before/after, targets attempted)
51	- For EACH target attempted:
52	  - **Every significant attempt** with: tactic/code tried, Lean error received, goal state at that point
53	  - What was learned from each failed attempt
54	  - For solved targets: the final proof structure with key lemmas
55	- Key findings / proof patterns discovered
56	- Recommendations for next session
57	
58	### File B: `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_<N>/milestones.jsonl`
59	
60	Each line MUST follow this JSON format — one entry per target theorem:
61	
62	```json
63	{
64	  "timestamp": "ISO-8601",
65	  "status": "solved|partial|blocked|not_started",
66	  "target": {
67	    "file": "path/to/File.lean",
68	    "theorem": "theorem_name"
69	  },
70	  "session": {
71	    "id": "session_N",
72	    "model": "model-name"
73	  },
74	  "findings": {
75	    "blocker": "description if blocked",
76	    "key_lemmas_used": ["lemma1", "lemma2"]
77	  },
78	  "attempts": [
79	    {
80	      "attempt": 1,
81	      "strategy": "what was tried",
82	      "code_tried": "actual Lean code or tactic",
83	      "line_number": "line number in the initial file where this code was attempted",
84	      "lean_error": "actual error message if failed",
85	      "goal_before": "the goal state before this attempt",
86	      "goal_after": "the goal state after this attempt",
87	      "result": "success|failed|partial",
88	      "insight": "what was learned from this attempt"
89	    }
90	  ],
91	  "next_steps": "..."
92	}
93	```
94	
95	**CRITICAL**: The `attempts` array must reflect ACTUAL attempts from the pre-processed data:
96	- If `attempts_raw.jsonl` shows 5 edits to a file, there should be multiple attempts recorded
97	- Each attempt must include `code_tried` (from edit events) and `lean_error` (from diagnostic events)
98	- Do NOT summarize multiple attempts as "tried various approaches" — list each one
99	
100	### File C: `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_<N>/recommendations.md`
101	
102	Write concrete recommendations for the next plan agent iteration:
103	- Which targets are closest to completion and should be prioritized
104	- Which approaches showed promise but need more work
105	- Which targets are blocked and why (the plan agent should NOT assign these)
106	- Any reusable proof patterns discovered
107	
108	If your analysis shows the prover has hit the exact same blocker for several consecutive iterations on the same target, you should explicitly instruct the Plan Agent to avoid retrying the same approach without putting more effort into understanding the underlying issue.
109	
110	## Step 5: Update PROJECT_STATUS.md
111	
112	Update (or create) `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md`:
113	
114	```markdown
115	# Project Status
116	
117	## Overall Progress
118	- **Total sorry**: <N>
119	- **Branches / Sub-goals closed this session**: <count> (Track real structural progress even if raw sorry count didn't drop)
120	- **Solved this session**: <list with file + theorem>
121	- **Partial**: <list with progress summary>
122	- **Blocked**: <list with reasons>
123	- **Untouched**: <list>
124	
125	## Knowledge Base
126	### Proof Patterns (reusable across targets)
127	- <pattern name>: <description + key lemmas>
128	
129	### Known Blockers (do not retry)
130	- <target>: <reason>
131	
132	## Last Updated
133	<ISO timestamp>
134	```
135	
136	## Step 6: Blueprint Markers
137	
138	`\leanok` placement is now handled deterministically by Archon's `sync_leanok` phase, which runs between the prover and you. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration in the Lean source, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. **Do not touch `\leanok` markers yourself.** If you see one missing where you expect it, the underlying file probably doesn't compile or still has a sorry — do not paper over that with a manual edit.
139	
140	Your remaining marker responsibilities are the ones that require semantic judgement:
141	
142	- **`\mathlibok`** (statement-block only) — declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias / direct reference). The deterministic script never adds or removes this; you decide based on the prover's task result.
143	- **`\lean{...}` macro maintenance** — if a prover renamed a declaration or chose a different name from the plan agent's hint, their task result will mention it. Update the `\lean{...}` line in the chapter to the correct Lean name. If a declaration was moved to a different file by a refactor, update the chapter's location references as needed.
144	- **`% NOTE: <reason>` annotations** — when a block is unformalized because the informal statement did not translate cleanly, add a `% NOTE: ...` comment explaining the obstacle so the plan agent sees it.
145	- **Stripping `\notready`** — if a `\notready` marker still sits on a block that the prover has now landed, remove it. The deterministic script does not manage `\notready`.
146	
147	### `\mathlibok` rules (your domain)
148	
149	Add `\mathlibok` inside the statement block when:
150	- The Lean side references a Mathlib name directly (`def foo := Mathlib.bar`, `theorem foo := Mathlib.bar`, or a simple `export Mathlib.Foo (bar)`), AND
151	- The Archon-side declaration itself contains no `sorry` and introduces no new proof obligation.
152	
153	If you add `\mathlibok`, no `\leanok` is needed on the proof block — the deterministic script will leave the proof block alone if there's no Lean proof body to verify.
154	
155	### Record what you changed
156	
157	In `session_<N>/summary.md`, include a "Blueprint markers updated" section listing **only the changes you personally made** (the deterministic script's `\leanok` adds/removes are committed separately as `archon[NNN/marker-sync]` and don't need to appear here):
158	
159	```markdown
160	## Blueprint markers updated (manual)
161	- `Algebra_WLocal.tex`, `lem:finite_closed`: added `\mathlibok` (backed by `Set.Finite.isClosed`)
162	- `Core.tex`, `thm:stacks_0A31`: added `% NOTE: prover reported translation gap, see task_results/Core.md`
163	- `Core.tex`, `thm:old_name`: stripped stale `\notready`
164	- `Core.tex`, `thm:foo`: corrected `\lean{Old.foo}` → `\lean{New.foo}` after refactor rename
165	```
166	
167	### File D: `.archon/TO_USER.md`
168	
169	While the agents should be autonomous, you might want to inform the user of any critical issues that require their attention. The content of `TO_USER.md` will be surfaced in the UI as an alert banner, however the user might not see it immediately or never see it at all (its content will be refreshed every iteration before the review agent is called). It might include for instance issues with the environment, critical missing dependencies, required user actions, etc. 
170	
171	**Rules:**
172	- Be extremely concise (1-2 sentences per item, listed in markdown format).
173	- If nothing relevant for the user is detected, leave the file completely empty.
174	
175	## Step 7: Self-Validation
176	
177	After writing all files, validate your output by checking:
178	- [ ] milestones.jsonl has valid JSON on every line
179	- [ ] Each milestone has `target.file`, `target.theorem`, `status`
180	- [ ] Each non-blocked milestone has at least 1 attempt with `code_tried` or `strategy`
181	- [ ] Number of attempts per milestone is proportional to edits in `attempts_raw.jsonl`
182	- [ ] summary.md includes specific code/errors, not just high-level summaries
183	- [ ] recommendations.md includes actionable next steps
184	- [ ] You did NOT add or remove any `\leanok` marker yourself (those are handled by the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase that ran before you)
185	- [ ] For every Mathlib-backed declaration the prover reported, the blueprint chapter has `\mathlibok` (your domain)
186	- [ ] Any `\lean{...}` macro rename flagged in a `task_results/<file>.md` has been applied
187	- [ ] No `\notready` marker remains on a block whose Lean declaration now exists
188	
189	## Permissions
190	
191	You may write to:
192	- `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_<N>/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
193	- `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md`
194	- `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` — markers (`\leanok`, `\mathlibok`), `\lean{...}` macro corrections, stale-marker cleanup, and `% NOTE:` comments. Do NOT rewrite the informal prose — that is the plan agent's surface.
195	
196	You must NOT write to:
197	- Any `.lean` files
198	- `.archon/PROGRESS.md` (plan agent's responsibility)
199	- `.archon/task_pending.md` or `.archon/task_done.md` (plan agent's responsibility)
200	- Blueprint informal content (theorem/proof prose, chapter structure) — only the markers and `% NOTE:` comments are yours.

1	# Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean
2	
3	## `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` body L589 trailing sorry (iter-094)
4	
5	### Iter-094 outcome summary
6	
7	**Status**: PARTIAL substantive progress. File compiles, 6 sorries (unchanged
8	from iter-093). Genuine breakthrough on the eqToHom-blocker, plus proved
9	body-local infrastructure (`key₂`) for the next step. Sorry budget hard cap
10	(6) maintained.
11	
12	### Step (b) / Route (D-variant) — BREAKTHROUGH
13	
14	**Committed line: `rw [← ModuleCat.hom_comp]` (L583).**
15	
16	The iter-094 plan's Routes (D)/(E)/(F) were all aimed at sidestepping the
17	iter-093 blocker: `(eqToHom_hom ∘ₗ (∑F).hom) z` couldn't be unfolded by
18	`LinearMap.comp_apply` due to def-equal-but-syntactically-distinct ModuleCat
19	mismatch (`∏ᶜ Z₂` vs Pi-product).
20	
21	**Discovery**: Mathlib's `ModuleCat.hom_comp f g : (f ≫ g).hom = g.hom ∘ₗ f.hom`
22	provides a `rfl`-lemma whose **reverse direction** (`← ModuleCat.hom_comp`)
23	converts `eqToHom_hom ∘ₗ (∑F).hom` into `((∑F) ≫ eqToHom).hom` — a categorical

173	## Key findings / proof patterns discovered
174	
175	### Pattern 1 — Per-application form of categorical sum-distribution
176	
177	The `have key₁ : ∀ F z, ((∑ i, F i) : ...).hom z = ∑ i, (F i).hom z`
178	pattern combines `ModuleCat.hom_sum` + `LinearMap.sum_apply` into a
179	single body-local statement keyed on BOTH the family `F` and the
180	input `z`. This avoids HOU on the discrimination tree because:
181	
182	- The LHS `((∑ F).hom z)` has both arguments syntactically present.
183	- `rw [key₁]` will fire when the goal contains the LHS shape directly,
184	  WITHOUT requiring sum-distribution to occur first.
185	
186	Proof body:
187	```lean
188	intro F z
189	rw [ModuleCat.hom_sum F Finset.univ]
190	exact LinearMap.sum_apply Finset.univ (fun i => (F i).hom) z
191	```
192	
193	**Reusability**: any project-local proof that needs to distribute
194	`.hom` over a `Finset.sum` in `ModuleCat k` context can adopt this
195	pattern. The two-argument keying is the cure for HOU obstructions
196	where the sum is wrapped in further LinearMap composition.
197	
198	### Pattern 2 — Diagnose `LinearMap.comp` HOU via `set_option pp.notation false in show True`
199	
200	When `simp only [LinearMap.comp_apply]` reports "no progress" but the
201	goal visually contains `(?f ∘ₗ ?g) ?x`, force the elaborator to print
202	the un-notated term via `set_option pp.notation false in show True`.
203	This reveals:
204	- whether `.comp` is `LinearMap.comp` (rfl-lemma applies) or some
205	  other composition;
206	- whether the implicit source/target ModuleCats reduce syntactically
207	  to the named ones from local context;
208	- whether an `eqToHom` is hiding a metavariable-driven cast.
209	
210	The diagnosis is what isolated the iter-093 sub-blocker
211	(`dif_pos hRel`-introduced eqToHom with non-syntactic source).
212	
213	### Pattern 3 — Mathlib `Preadditive.sum_comp` / `Preadditive.comp_sum`
214	
215	When ModuleCat `∘ₗ`-unfolding fails because of eqToHom-source HOU,
216	the categorical-level alternative is `(∑ F) ≫ Pi.π Z₂ j = ∑ (F i ≫
217	Pi.π Z₂ j)` via `Preadditive.sum_comp` (Mathlib
218	`CategoryTheory/Preadditive/Basic.lean` L185). Verified to exist this
219	iteration. Requires the proof to operate at categorical level BEFORE
220	the iter-088 dsimp at L526–L535 unfolds to LinearMap form. Reserved
221	for iter-094 Route (F).

119	## Reusable proof patterns discovered
120	
121	### Per-application form for ModuleCat sum-distribution
122	
123	Whenever a project-local proof needs to distribute `(∑F).hom` over a
124	sum, prefer the 2-argument keyed form:
125	```lean
126	have key : ∀ (F : ι → (M ⟶ N)) (z : ↑M), ((∑ i, F i) : M ⟶ N).hom z = ∑ i, (F i).hom z := by
127	  intro F z
128	  rw [ModuleCat.hom_sum F Finset.univ]
129	  exact LinearMap.sum_apply Finset.univ (fun i => (F i).hom) z
130	```
131	over the 1-argument `(∑F).hom = ∑ (F i).hom` form. The two-argument
132	keying gives `rw` enough fixed positions to bind both sides of the
133	discrimination tree.
134	
135	### Diagnostic `pp.notation false`
136	
137	When a rfl-lemma rewrite fails inexplicably, use `set_option
138	pp.notation false in show True` BEFORE the failing rewrite to print
139	the goal in un-notated form. Hidden `eqToHom`-casts, implicit
140	ModuleCat-types, and `dif_pos`-introduced metavariables become visible.
141	This pattern resolved iter-093's debugging in ~3 minutes.

229	  iter-093)*: when distributing `(∑F).hom` over a Finset.sum in

233	      ((∑ i, F i) : M ⟶ N).hom z = ∑ i, (F i).hom z := by

captures the canonical distribution shape and can be applied via
`convert` or by manual `Finset.cons_induction` as a fallback.

**localises** the obstruction precisely enough that the iter-095 plan
can target either (a) `Finset.cons_induction` to bypass HOU entirely,
or (b) renaming the inner binder to escape shadowing.

**Route (D′) `convert key₂ _ _ _ using ...` is the recommended starting
point** — smallest delta on top of the iter-094 commit.

## Priority targets for iter-095

### Priority 1: `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` step (c) — Route (D′) `convert`

**Approach**: bypass the discrimination tree's strict HOU unification
by using `convert key₂ G E z using n` at the post-(b') goal. `convert`
matches up to definitional equality and generates equality subgoals
for any non-matching positions; those subgoals should then be
dischargeable by `rfl`, `ext`, or trivial elaboration.

**Why this is recommended**:
- **Minimal delta**: iter-094 already proved `key₂` and committed the
  `rw [← ModuleCat.hom_comp]` breakthrough. Just add a `convert`
  invocation immediately after, no new helpers.
- **HOU-tolerant**: `convert` doesn't use the discrimination tree
  matcher; it does up-to-defeq comparison structurally.
- **Trade-off**: `convert` may leave several subgoals if the LHS and
  RHS differ in places where `key₂`'s unifier had degrees of freedom.
  Each subgoal will need explicit closure (often `rfl` or `ext`).

**Concrete tactic sequence** (paste into `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` at the
post-`have key₂` position L589):
```lean
-- Route (D′): convert against key₂ to bypass HOU; expect ≤4 subgoals
convert key₂ (fun i ↦ (-1)^(↑i : ℤ) • Pi.lift fun i_1 ↦ ...) _ _ using 2
-- subgoals 1..N: equalities at non-matching positions, mostly rfl / ext
all_goals first | rfl | (ext; rfl) | sorry
```

If the `convert` arity-2 invocation doesn't elaborate cleanly because
of `(-1)^↑i` heterogeneous-power inference, try a Finset.cons_induction
fallback (Route D″ below) instead.

### Priority 2 (parallel/fallback): Route (D″) `Finset.cons_induction`

**Approach**: manually distribute the sum over `Finset.univ : Finset
(Fin (n+1))` via `Finset.cons_induction`. This avoids HOU entirely
because each induction step handles a single summand, not a sum-pattern.

**Why this is a viable fallback**:
- Zero pattern-matching involved — induction reduces the goal step by
  step.
- Works even when the summand has the worst binder-shadowing because
  each step instantiates `i` to a concrete `Fin (n+1)` value.
- Mathlib provides `Finset.cons_induction` and `Finset.induction_on`
  with the appropriate signatures.

**Concrete tactic sequence**:
```lean
-- Route (D″): Finset.cons_induction over Finset.univ
induction (Finset.univ : Finset (Fin (n+1))) using Finset.cons_induction with
| empty =>
    simp only [Finset.sum_empty, ModuleCat.hom_zero, Pi.zero_apply, smul_zero]
| cons i s hi ih =>
    rw [Finset.sum_cons, Finset.sum_cons]
    rw [Preadditive.add_comp, ModuleCat.hom_add, LinearMap.add_apply,
        Pi.π_apply, map_add, smul_add]
    rw [ih]
    ring -- or `congr 1`
```

**Predicted blockers**: the `s` (rest-of-Finset) case may still need a
helper if `Preadditive.add_comp` doesn't fire on the eqToHom-wrapped
side. In that case, fall back to Route (D‴).

### Priority 3 (only if D′ and D″ fail): Route (D‴) `Preadditive.comp_sum`

**Approach**: use the DUAL of `Preadditive.sum_comp` to push
`Pi.π Z₂ j` INSIDE each summand's `≫`-composition BEFORE
`.hom`-application:
```
(Pi.π Z₂ j) ∘ ((∑F) ≫ eqToHom)
  = ((∑F) ≫ eqToHom) ≫ Pi.π Z₂ j   -- (rewrite from Pi.π_apply or similar)
  = ∑ i, F i ≫ eqToHom ≫ Pi.π Z₂ j -- via Preadditive.sum_comp on the right of ≫
```

The post-`Preadditive.comp_sum` form has the outer `(Pi.π Z₂ j).hom`
**inside** each summand's categorical composition, which is the most
robust shape for downstream `.hom`-distribution and per-summand
`Pi.lift_π` collapse.

**Why this is a fallback, not a primary**: requires more careful
setup; the iter-094 breakthrough was specifically a LinearMap → categorical
re-folding, and this dual move requires another re-folding step. If
the iter-094 commit's `rw [← ModuleCat.hom_comp]` is positioned to
make this work, fine — otherwise this route involves restructuring
the proof prefix.

## Do NOT retry in iter-095 (carried from session_93 + NEW from iter-094)

The plan agent should explicitly enumerate these in `PROGRESS.md`'s
"Dead-ends to avoid" section:

- **All `LinearMap.comp_apply` / `LinearMap.coe_comp` / `Function.comp_apply`
  variants on the pre-(b') goal** — iter-093 dead-end CONFIRMED iter-094
  via 7 additional variant probes (including `dsimp only`,
  `rw [show ... from fun ... => rfl]` rfl-witness, `change _ = _; simp`).
  All fail with "no progress" / "pattern `(?f ∘ₛₗ ?g) ?x` not found".
  Use `rw [← ModuleCat.hom_comp]` (iter-094 breakthrough) instead.
- **`rw [Preadditive.sum_comp]` directly on the post-(b') goal** —
  NEW DEAD-END (iter-094). HOU fails on `(∑ j ∈ ?s, ?f j) ≫ ?g`
  pattern because the summand body has binder-shadowing on `i`. Use
  Route (D′) `convert key₂` or Route (D″) `Finset.cons_induction`.
- **`simp only [Preadditive.sum_comp, ModuleCat.hom_sum, LinearMap.sum_apply,
  map_sum, Finset.smul_sum]` (ensemble simp)** — NEW DEAD-END
  (iter-094). HOU pre-filter rejects the first lemma; cascade doesn't
  fire.
- **`rw [key₂]` / `simp_rw [key₂]` directly on the post-(b') goal** —
  NEW DEAD-END (iter-094). The `key₂` helper IS PROVED CORRECTLY but
  its `rw` application HOU-fails for the same reason direct
  `Preadditive.sum_comp` does. **Use `convert key₂ _ _ _` instead.**
- **`have key₃` bundling outer `(Pi.π Z₂ jj).hom`** — NEW DEAD-END
  (iter-094). `(Pi.π Z₂ jj).hom` doesn't accept `(G i ≫ E).hom z`-typed
  input directly because its codomain is the Pi-product (def-equal
  but not syntactically equal to `∏ᶜ Z₂`); the eqToHom-source obstruction
  resurfaces one layer up.
- **`set F :=` to name the summand and bypass HOU** — NEW DEAD-END
  (iter-094). `(-1)^↑i` heterogeneous-power inference fails outside
  the local `Finset.sum` context; Pi.π type mismatch.
- **`simp_rw [hom_sum_dist]` / `simp_rw [ModuleCat.hom_sum]`** —
  iter-093 dead-end (still applicable).
- **`have key₁` index-specialised (1-argument form) followed by
  `rw [key₁]`** — iter-093 dead-end.
- **`show ...` / `change ...` with explicit eqToHom source** —
  iter-093 dead-end.
- **`rw [show ... from ModuleCat.hom_sum f Finset.univ]` with concrete
  type `Fin (n+1) → (∏ᶜ Z₁ ⟶ ∏ᶜ Z₂)`** — iter-092 dead-end.

## Reusable proof patterns discovered

### Pattern 1: Reverse-direction `← ModuleCat.hom_comp` for eqToHom-wrapped `∘ₗ` (NEW iter-094)

When a goal contains `eqToHom_hom ∘ₗ f.hom` shape and
`LinearMap.comp_apply` fails because the `eqToHom`'s source ModuleCat
is metavariable-driven, use `rw [← ModuleCat.hom_comp]` to re-fold
the `∘ₗ` into a **categorical composition** `(f ≫ eqToHom).hom`.
Categorical types are stable under unification (no LinearMap-level
metavariable HOU). This pattern resolves the iter-093 `∘ₗ`-unfolding
blocker in **one line**. Reusable across the project anywhere
`LinearMap.comp_apply` reports "pattern `(?f ∘ₛₗ ?g) ?x` not found"
on a `(eqToHom_hom ∘ₗ X).hom z` goal.

### Pattern 2: Body-local FREE-variable distributor for Preadditive sums (NEW iter-094)

For ModuleCat-level `((∑ G) ≫ E).hom z = ∑ (G i ≫ E).hom z`:
```lean
have key : ∀ G E z, ((∑ i, G i) ≫ E).hom z = ∑ i, (G i ≫ E).hom z := by
  intro G E z
  rw [Preadditive.sum_comp]
  rw [ModuleCat.hom_sum (fun i => G i ≫ E) Finset.univ]
  exact LinearMap.sum_apply Finset.univ (fun i => (G i ≫ E).hom) z
```
The proof always succeeds (FREE variables = no HOU). Application via
`rw` may HOU-fail on the call site if the summand has binder-shadowing;
prefer `convert` or `Finset.cons_induction` in that case.

### Pattern 3: HOU localisation via binder-shadow audit (NEW iter-094)

When `rw [helper]` HOU-fails on a sum-pattern with no apparent
structural mismatch, audit the summand body for:
- **References to the outer summation binder at nested depths** (e.g.,
  `... fun j ↦ ... ((SimplexCategory.δ i) j) ...` where `i` is the
  outer binder).
- **Binder shadowing** (e.g., `Pi.π (fun i ↦ ...)` re-binding the same
  letter as the outer `i`).
- **Heterogeneous coercions on the binder** (e.g., `(-1)^(↑i : ℤ)`
  where `i : Fin (n+1)`).

These three features defeat Lean's HOU pattern matcher. Fix by
(a) renaming the inner shadowing binder, (b) using `convert` /
`Finset.cons_induction` to avoid HOU, or (c) reformulating the helper
to take an explicit `i` parameter rather than a sum-pattern.

## Knowledge Base

### Proof Patterns (reusable across targets)

- **Reverse-direction `← ModuleCat.hom_comp` to escape LinearMap-level
  eqToHom HOU** *(NEW iter-094, BREAKTHROUGH)*: when a goal contains
  `eqToHom_hom ∘ₗ f.hom` and `LinearMap.comp_apply` fails because the
  `eqToHom`'s source ModuleCat is metavariable-driven (def-equal but
  not syntactically the named target), use `rw [← ModuleCat.hom_comp]`
  to re-fold the `∘ₗ` into a categorical composition `(f ≫ eqToHom).hom`.
  Categorical types are stable under unification (no LinearMap-level
  metavariable HOU). One-line fix; resolves the iter-093 `∘ₗ`-unfolding
  blocker. Reusable anywhere `LinearMap.comp_apply` reports `pattern
  (?f ∘ₛₗ ?g) ?x not found` on `(eqToHom_hom ∘ₗ X).hom z`. See
  iter-094 `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` L570.
- **Body-local FREE-variable distributor for categorical Preadditive
  sums** *(NEW iter-094)*: `((∑ G) ≫ E).hom z = ∑ (G i ≫ E).hom z`
  packaged as
  ```lean
  have key : ∀ G E z, ((∑ i, G i) ≫ E).hom z = ∑ i, (G i ≫ E).hom z := by
    intro G E z
    rw [Preadditive.sum_comp]
    rw [ModuleCat.hom_sum (fun i => G i ≫ E) Finset.univ]
    exact LinearMap.sum_apply Finset.univ (fun i => (G i ≫ E).hom) z
  ```
  The proof always succeeds (FREE variables = no HOU). Application via
  `rw [key]` may HOU-fail at the call site if the summand has
  binder-shadowing; use `convert key _ _ _` or `Finset.cons_induction`
  in that case. See iter-094 `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` L580–L588.
- **HOU-blocker localisation via binder-shadow audit** *(NEW iter-094,
  debugging)*: when `rw [helper]` HOU-fails on a sum-pattern with no
  apparent structural mismatch, audit the summand body for (a) outer-
  binder references at nested depths (e.g., `... fun j ↦ ... ((SimplexCategory.δ i) j) ...`),
  (b) binder shadowing (`Pi.π (fun i ↦ ...)` re-binding the same
  letter as the outer `i`), (c) heterogeneous coercions on the binder
  (`(-1)^(↑i : ℤ)` where `i : Fin (n+1)`). These three features
  defeat Lean's HOU pattern matcher. Fix by renaming the inner
  shadowing binder, using `convert` / `Finset.cons_induction` to avoid
  HOU, or reformulating the helper to take an explicit `i` parameter.
- **Per-application form of categorical sum-distribution** *(NEW
  iter-093)*:

- **`LinearMap.comp_apply` / `LinearMap.coe_comp + Function.comp_apply`
  / `rw [LinearMap.comp_apply]` / `dsimp only [LinearMap.comp_apply]`
  / `rw [show ... from fun ... => rfl]` rfl-witness variant on
  `(eqToHom ∘ₗ (∑F).hom) z`** *(iter-093 + iter-094 confirmed dead-end
  across 7+ variants)*: all variants fail with `simp made no progress`
  or `pattern not found `(?g ∘ₗ ?h) ?x``. Root cause: the `eqToHom`
  factor's source ModuleCat is metavariable-driven (from `dif_pos hRel`
  at L535) and is NOT syntactically `∏ᶜ Z₂` — it's a def-equal
  unfolding like `ModuleCat.of k ((i : Fin (n+1) → ↥s₀) → ↑(Z₂ i))`.
  The discrimination-tree LHS pattern `(?f ∘ₗ ?g) ?x` cannot bind
  `?g`'s codomain. **RESOLVED iter-094**: use `rw [← ModuleCat.hom_comp]`
  instead to re-fold the `∘ₗ` into the categorical `((∑F) ≫ eqToHom).hom z`
  form (see Proof Patterns).
- **`rw [Preadditive.sum_comp]` / `simp only [Preadditive.sum_comp]` /
  `simp_rw [Preadditive.sum_comp]` / `rw [key₂]` / `simp only [key₂]`
  / `simp_rw [key₂]` / combined-simp-ensemble
  `[Preadditive.sum_comp, ModuleCat.hom_sum, LinearMap.sum_apply, map_sum, Finset.smul_sum]`
  on the post-(b') goal** *(NEW iter-094 HOU blocker)*: all variants
  fail with `pattern (∑ j ∈ ?s, ?f j) ≫ ?g not found` or `simp made no
  progress`. Root cause: the summand body
  `(-1)^↑i • Pi.lift fun i_1 ↦ ... ((SimplexCategory.δ i) ...) ...`
  references the outer summation binder `i` at NESTED binding depths
  AND there is binder shadowing (inner `Pi.π (fun i ↦ ...)` rebinds
  the same letter `i`). Lean's HOU pattern matcher cannot determine
  the `?G` placeholder. **Recovery routes (iter-095)**: (D′)
  `convert key₂ _ _ _ using n` to bypass the discrimination tree
  (RECOMMENDED); (D″) `Finset.cons_induction` to avoid HOU entirely;
  (D‴) `Preadditive.comp_sum` (dual) to push `Pi.π Z₂ j` inside each
  summand BEFORE `.hom`-application.
- **`have key₃` bundling outer `(Pi.π Z₂ jj).hom`** *(NEW iter-094)*:
  `(Pi.π Z₂ jj).hom` doesn't accept `(G i ≫ E).hom z`-typed input
  directly because its codomain is the Pi-product (def-equal but not
  syntactically equal to `∏ᶜ Z₂`); the eqToHom-source obstruction
  resurfaces one layer up. Use `convert` / `Finset.cons_induction` /
  `Preadditive.comp_sum` instead.
- **`set F :=` to name the summand and bypass HOU** *(NEW iter-094)*:
  `(-1)^↑i` heterogeneous-power inference fails outside the local
  `Finset.sum` context; Pi.π type mismatch.
